tool-checkpoint: tool-checkpoint: hook-enforced retry point. hook-stage: PreToolUse. meaning: tool-call retry checkpoint, not user-input intake. Emit a visible [tool-checkpoint] block on every tool call, always carrying at least intent and why. For a read-only tool call (Read, Grep, Glob, or read-only inspection) with no active boundary-contract and no forced signal, intent and why are sufficient. Add contract-ref and contract-check when a boundary-contract is active. Add procedure, localized-human-note, rejected-alternatives, unverified-premises, and failure-mode-if-wrong when the tool has side effects (Write, Edit, Bash) or a forced signal is present (prior nonzero exit, security boundary, or verification failure). Add recovery-action only when a mismatch, scope reopen, external side effect, or hard-to-recover action needs a concrete next step. If mismatch changes scope, state focus-layer and scope-reopen target in procedure or recovery-action. These tiers follow the observable tool kind and the recorded boundary and forced state, not a self-judgment that the call is safe; never skip the gate itself. Keep field names and literal tokens in English; write human notes in the current user language.
